headline: Anthropic's Fable 5 launched with a 2x price premium that dominated the quarter's model-evaluation chatter while workflow automation and operational efficiency rotated up.
body: Anthropic released Fable 5 (also called Mythos 5) at a 2x premium over Claude Opus 4.8, drawing live tests and benchmark comparisons against GPT 5.5 alongside user complaints that it burned 45% of a weekly quota in a single day [1][10][11]. Anthropic separately reversed a policy that would have quietly limited Claude on frontier LLM development, citing the wrong tradeoff between transparency and safeguards [9]. On the rotation side, workflow automation and operational efficiency are improving on rising momentum while multi-model chaining and neural networks weaken, and agent dispatch via MCP and git worktrees shows where the practical attention is moving [0][2]. NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 and Groq's LPU inference work round out the hardware and architecture thread, framed through Jevons Paradox and cheaper compute [3][5].
